objects.name: FamousSparrow
objects.description: PRC-attributed threat actor conducting long-term cyber espionage campaigns against hotel, telecom, and government infrastructure globally. Attribution confirmed by FBI/CISA joint advisory (2024). Operates in UTC+8 (China Standard Time).

objects.name: SparrowDoor
objects.description: Legacy variant (2019-2022). Stage 1 loader component. Masquerades as K7 Antivirus component (K7UI.dll). Loads Stage 2 (MpSvc.dll) via reflective DLL injection. Minimal import profile (KERNEL32.dll only). Features inverted anti-sandbox logic terminating on IsDebuggerPresent==0. Three-table character substitution system for API-free case-insensitive command processing.

objects.name: SparrowDoor Stage 1 Loader -- K7UI.dll
objects.description: SHA256 hash of SparrowDoor Stage 1 loader component masquerading as K7 Antivirus. PE32 DLL, 32-bit Intel i386, Windows 5.00 subsystem. Falsified export table asserting K7 identity (K7UI_1, K7UI_13). Source: ESET malware-ioc.

objects.name: SparrowDoor C2 -- 194.165.16.69
objects.description: SparrowDoor Stage 2 C2 server IP. Source: ESET malware-ioc. Not independently verified by SerapHim.

objects.name: SparrowDoor C2 -- 198.12.75.112
objects.description: SparrowDoor Stage 2 C2 server IP. Source: ESET malware-ioc. Not independently verified by SerapHim.

objects.name: DRBControl Metasploit C2 on FamousSparrow victim -- cdn.kkxx888666.com
objects.description: Domain observed as Metasploit C2 on a FamousSparrow-compromised machine. Attributed to DRBControl group. Defanged in report: cdn.kkxx888666[.]com. Indicates tool-sharing or contractor overlap between FamousSparrow and DRBControl.

objects.name: SparrowDoor DLL sideloading masquerade -- K7UI.dll export pattern
objects.description: Falsified export table with K7UI_1 and K7UI_13 exports asserting K7 Antivirus identity. K7 Computing primary market: India, Southeast Asia, MENA. Export naming pattern is YARA candidate for Asia/MENA variant tracking.

objects.name: [PHIM] Inverted anti-sandbox -- TerminateProcess on IsDebuggerPresent==0
objects.description: SparrowDoor terminates via TerminateProcess with exception code 0xc0000409 (STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN) when IsDebuggerPresent returns 0 (no debugger detected). Inverted from conventional anti-debug: malware runs normally when debugger IS present (sandbox environments attach no debugger). Masquerades deliberate termination as compiler security feature. Entry: DllMain at 0x100016a1, anti-sandbox logic at FUN_100016c4. Not documented in prior public vendor reporting at time of publication.

objects.name: DLL Side-Loading (T1574.002)
objects.description: K7UI.dll masquerading as K7 Antivirus component via falsified export table.

objects.name: Obfuscated Files or Information (T1027)
objects.description: Three-table character substitution system in .rdata (0x10008898, 0x10008ea1, 0x10009021). .text section entropy 6.59/8.0 (anomalous). 113 indirect call sites (FF 15) masking runtime API footprint.

objects.name: Process Injection: DLL Injection (T1055.001)
objects.description: Reflective loading of MpSvc.dll into memory via VirtualAlloc + VirtualProtect + manual PE parsing. Stage 2 never written to disk as standalone file.

objects.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools (T1562.001)
objects.description: Inverted anti-sandbox logic via IsDebuggerPresent. Process terminates with STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN (0xc0000409) when no debugger is detected, defeating automated sandbox analysis.

objects.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information (T1140)
objects.description: Runtime decryption via three-table substitution system. AES decryption of Stage 2 payload. XOR key hH7@83#mi for C2 communication (Stage 2, UK NCSC).

objects.name: Command Interpreter: Windows Command Shell (T1059.003)
objects.description: Stage 2 remote command execution via Windows Command Shell. Source: external reporting (UK NCSC, ESET).

objects.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols (T1071.001)
objects.description: C2 communication over HTTPS port 443. Source: UK NCSC.
